name: d74ef9cf-0114-4651-8844-c7fd21e0f160
description:
This tool schedules events based on user input. It can create a simple one-time event or a more complex repeating event series.

For a single event, it requires the event name, type, start date/time, and optionally an end date/time.

To create a repeating event, the tool must be provided with the repetition details, such as: frequency, interval, and an end date.

The function uses these details to generate all the individual event instances for the user's calendar.

endDateTime.description: Optional. The ending date and time of the event in ISO 8601 format. Required if the event is not all-day.

eventName.description: The name or title of the event.

eventType.description: The category or type of the event (e.g., 'Meeting', 'Appointment', 'Birthday').

isAllDay.description: Set to true if the event lasts for the entire day. Defaults to false.

isFromPlaybook.description: Whether or not this call is from a dialogflow playbook.

isRepeating.description: Set to true if this is a recurring event. If true, the 'repetition' object is required.

repetition.description: Contains the rules for a repeating event. Required if 'isRepeating' is true.

interval.description: The gap between repetitions (e.g., an interval of 2 with a 'Weekly' frequency means every 2 weeks).

monthlyType.description: For 'Monthly' frequency, specifies how to repeat. 'dayOfWeek' repeats on the same week and day (e.g., the fourth Friday).

weekdays.description: For 'Weekly' frequency, an array of numbers representing days of the week (0=Sunday, 1=Monday, ..., 6=Saturday).

repetitionEndDate.description: Optional. The date when the repeating event series ends, in ISO 8601 format.

startDate.description: The starting date and time of the event in ISO 8601 format (e.g., '2023-10-27T10:00:00Z').

timeZone.description: An IANA time zone representing the user's current timezone

message.description: A human-readable message describing the outcome, for example, 'Single event created.' or 'Repeating event series created.'

status.description: Indicates the result of the operation, typically 'success'.
